name: InNat

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add a `Nat (N ...)`.

    between70And100
        |> InNat.add nat7
    --> : Nat (In Nat77 (Nat107Plus a_))

Use [addIn](InNat#addIn) if you want to add a `Nat` that isn't a `Nat (N ...)`.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) (Nat.Is max Nat.To sumMax)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In sumMin sumMax)

values.name: addIn
values.comment:
Add a `Nat` that isn't a `Nat (N ...)`.

The first 2 arguments are

  - the minimum added value

  - the maximum added value

```
between3And10
    |> InNat.addIn nat1 nat12 between1And12
--> : Nat (In Nat4 (Nat22Plus a_))
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N minAdded atLeastMinAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) minAddedIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N maxAdded atLeastMaxAdded_ (Nat.Is max Nat.To sumMax) maxAddedIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minAdded maxAdded addedIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In sumMin sumMax)

values.name: atLeast
values.comment:
Return the given number if the `Nat` is less.

    between5And9 |> InNat.atLeast nat10
    --> Nat 10 : Nat (In Nat10 (Nat10Plus a_))

    nat15 |> InNat.atLeast nat10
    --> Nat 15
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minNewMin max lowerIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min_ max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In minNewMin max)

values.name: is
values.comment:
Is the `Nat` `LessOrEqualOrGreater` than a given number?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum.

    giveAPresent { age } =
        case age |> InNat.is nat18 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.Less age ->
                toy { age = age }

            Nat.Greater age ->
                experience { age = age }

            Nat.Equal _ ->
                bigPresent

    toy : { age : Nat (ArgIn min Nat17 ifN_) } -> Toy

    experience :
        { age : Nat (ArgIn (Nat19Plus orOlder) max ifN_) }
        -> Experience
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N (N.Nat1Plus valueMinus1) atLeastValue (Nat.Is a_ Nat.To (N.Nat1Plus atLeastValueMinus1)) (Nat.Is valueToMax_ Nat.To max)) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToValue_ Nat.To (N.Nat1Plus valueMinus1))) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.LessOrEqualOrGreater (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowest atLeastValueMinus1)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In (N.Nat1Plus valueMinus1) atLeastValue)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In (N.Nat2Plus valueMinus1) max))

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat` `BelowOrAtLeast` as big as a given number?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum.

    vote :
        { age : Nat (ArgIn (Nat18Plus orOlder) max ifN_) }
        -> Vote

    tryToVote { age } =
        case age |> InNat.isAtLeast nat18 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.Below _ ->
                --😓
                Nothing

            Nat.EqualOrGreater age ->
                Just (vote { age = age })
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowerBound (N.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) (Nat.Is atLeastRange_ Nat.To max) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest (Nat.Is lowestToMin Nat.To min) (Nat.Is (N.Nat1Plus lowestToLowerBound_) Nat.To lowerBound)) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.BelowOrAtLeast (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowest atLeastLowerBoundMinus1)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowerBound max))

values.name: isAtMost
values.comment:
Is the `Nat` `AtMostOrAbove` a given number?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum.

    goToU18Party : { age : Nat (ArgIn min Nat17 ifN_) } -> Snack

    tryToGoToU18Party { age } =
        case age |> InNat.isAtMost nat17 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.EqualOrLess age ->
                Just (goToU18Party { age = age })

            Nat.Above ->
                Nothing
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N upperBound atLeastUpperBound (Nat.Is (N.Nat1Plus greaterRange_) Nat.To max) is_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToUpperBound_ Nat.To upperBound)) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.AtMostOrAbove (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowest atLeastUpperBound)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In (N.Nat1Plus upperBound) max))

values.name: isInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is the `Nat` `BelowOrInOrAboveRange`?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum.

    justIfBetween3And10 nat =
        case nat |> InNat.isInRange nat3 nat10 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.InRange ->
                Just

            _ ->
                Nothing

    justIfBetween3And10 nat9
    --> Just (Nat 9)

    justIfBetween3And10 nat123
    --> Nothing
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowerBound (N.Nat1Plus atLeastLowerBoundMinus1) (Nat.Is lowerBoundToUpperBound_ Nat.To upperBound) lowerBoundIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N upperBound atLeastUpperBound (Nat.Is upperBoundToMax_ Nat.To max) upperBoundIs_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is minToLowerBound_ Nat.To lowerBound)) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.BelowOrInOrAboveRange (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowest atLeastLowerBoundMinus1)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowerBound atLeastUpperBound)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.In (N.Nat1Plus upperBound) max))

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Nat`s within a lower & upper bound.

    import Serialize

    serializePercent :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Nat (In Nat0 (Nat100Plus a_)))
    serializePercent =
        InNat.serialize nat0 nat100

    encode : Nat (ArgIn min_ Nat100 ifN_) -> Bytes
    encode =
        InNat.value
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serializePercent

    decode :
        Bytes
        ->
            Result
                (Serialize.Error String)
                (Nat (In Nat0 (Nat100Plus a_)))
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializePercent

For decoded `Int`s out of the expected bounds, the `Result` is an error message.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound maxLowerBound lowerBoundIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn maxLowerBound maxUpperBound upperBoundIfN_) -> Serialize.Codec String.String (Nat.Nat (Nat.In minLowerBound maxUpperBound))

values.comment:
Subtract an exact `Nat (N ...)` value.

    between7And10
        |> InNat.sub nat7
    --> : Nat (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a_))

Use [subIn](InNat#subIn) if you want to subtract a `Nat` that isn't a `Nat (N ...)`.

values.comment:
Subtract a `Nat` taht isn't a `Nat (N ...)`.

The first 2 arguments are

  - the minimum subtracted value

  - the maximum subtracted value

```
between6And12
    |> InNat.subIn nat1 nat5 between1And5
--> : Nat (In Nat1 (Nat5Plus a_))
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N minSubbed atLeastMinSubbed_ (Nat.Is differenceMax Nat.To max) minSubbedIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N maxSubbed atLeastMaxSubbed_ (Nat.Is differenceMin Nat.To min) maxSubbedIs_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minSubbed maxSubbed subbedIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In differenceMin differenceMax)

values.name: value
values.comment:
Convert it to a `Nat (In min max)`.

    InNat.value nat4
    --> : Nat (In Nat4 (Nat4Plus a_))

Example

    [ in3To10, nat3 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (In Nat3 Nat10)`

    [ in3To10
    , nat3 |> InNat.value
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In min max)

name: MinNat
comment:
2 situations where you use these operations instead of the ones in [`Nat`](Nat) or [`InNat`](InNat):

1.  Your value is of type `Nat (Min ...)`

        nat3 |> Nat.mul nat3
        --> Nat (Min Nat3)

2.  The maximum is a type variable

        divideBy : Nat (ArgIn (Nat1Plus minMinus1) max ifN_) -> --...
        divideBy atLeast1 =
            --...


# compare

@docs is, isAtLeast, isAtMost, atLeast


# modify

@docs sub, add, subMax, addMin


# drop information

@docs value


# extra

@docs serialize

values.name: add
values.comment:
Add an exact `Nat (N ...)` value.

    atLeast70 |> InNat.add nat7
    --> : Nat (Min Nat77)

Use [addMin](MinNat#addMin) if you want to add a `Nat` that isn't a `Nat (N ...)`.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.N added_ atLeastAdded_ (Nat.Is min Nat.To sumMin) is_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Min sumMin)

values.name: addMin
values.comment:
Add a `Nat` that isn't a `Nat (N ...)`. The second argument is the minimum added value.

    --> : Nat (Min Nat10)

values.comment:
Is the `Nat` `LessOrEqualOrGreater` than a given number?

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum.

    giveAPresent { age } =
        case age |> MinNat.is nat18 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.Less age ->
                toy { age = age }

            Nat.Greater age ->
                experience { age = age }

            Nat.Equal _ ->

    toy : { age : Nat (ArgIn Nat0 Nat17 ifN_) } -> Toy

values.name: isAtLeast
values.comment:
Is the `Nat` `BelowOrAtLeast` a given number?

    factorial : Nat (ArgIn min_ max_ ifN_) -> Nat (Min Nat1)
    factorial =
        factorialBody

    factorialBody : -- as in factorial
    factorialBody =
        case x |> MinNat.isAtLeast nat1 { lowest = nat0 } of
            Nat.Below _ ->
                MinNat.value nat1

            Nat.EqualOrGreater atLeast1 ->
                Nat.mul atLeast1
                    (factorial
                        (atLeast1 |> MinNat.sub nat1)
                    )

values.name: serialize
values.comment:
A [`Codec`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/MartinSStewart/elm-serialize/latest/) to serialize `Nat`s with a lower bound.

    import Serialize

    serializeNaturalNumber :
        Serialize.Codec
            String
            (Nat (Min Nat0))
    serializeNaturalNumber =
        MinNat.serialize nat0

    encode : Nat (ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Bytes
    encode =
        MinNat.value
            >> Serialize.encodeToBytes serializeNaturalNumber

    decode :
        Bytes
        -> Result (Serialize.Error String) (Nat (Min Nat0))
    decode =
        Serialize.decodeFromBytes serializeNaturalNumber

    --> : Nat (Min Nat1)

values.name: value
values.comment:
Convert a `Nat (ArgIn min ...)` to a `Nat (Min min)`.

    between3And10 |> MinNat.value
    --> : Nat (Min Nat4)

There is **only 1 situation you should use this.**

To make these the same type.

    [ atLeast1, between1And10 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (Min Nat1)`

    [ atLeast1
    , between1And10 |> MinNat.value
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Min min)

name: NNat

values.name: add
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus another `Nat (N ...)`. Give the added value twice as a tuple.

    nat6 |> NNat.add ( nat5, nat5 )
    --> Nat 11
    --> : Nat
    -->     (N Nat11
    -->         (Nat11Plus a)
    -->         (Is a To Nat11Plus a)
    -->         (Is b To Nat11Plus b)
    -->     )

This is only rarely useful, as you shouldn't

    inRangeXAndXPlus10 x =
        -- won't work
        isInRange x (x |> NNat.add ( nat10, nat10 ))

    isAtLeast10GreaterThan x =
        -- the only use case
        isAtLeast (x |> NNat.add ( nat10, nat10 ))

(examples don't compile, just for demonstration)
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastFirstAdded_ (Nat.Is n Nat.To sum) (Nat.Is atLeastN Nat.To atLeastSum)), Nat.Nat (Nat.N added atLeastSecondAdded_ (Nat.Is aPlusN Nat.To aPlusSum) (Nat.Is bPlusN Nat.To bPlusSum)) ) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N n atLeastN (Nat.Is a Nat.To aPlusN) (Nat.Is b Nat.To bPlusN)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N sum atLeastSum (Nat.Is a Nat.To aPlusSum) (Nat.Is b Nat.To bPlusSum))

values.name: sub
values.comment:
The `Nat (N ...)` plus another `Nat (N ...)`. Give the subtracted value twice as a tuple.

    nat6 |> NNat.sub ( nat5, nat5 )
    --> Nat 1
    --> : Nat
    -->     (N Nat1 (Nat1Plus orMore_)
    -->         (Is a To (Nat1Plus a))
    -->         (Is b To (Nat1Plus b))
    -->     )

This is only rarely useful, as you shouldn't

    inRangeXMinus10ToX x =
        -- won't work
        isInRange (x |> NNat.sub ( nat10, nat10 )) x

    isAtLeast10LessThan x =
        -- the only use case
        isAtLeast (x |> NNat.sub ( nat10, nat10 ))

(examples don't compile, just for demonstration)
values.type: ( Nat.Nat (Nat.N subbed atLeastFirstSubbed_ (Nat.Is difference Nat.To n) (Nat.Is atLeastDifference Nat.To atLeastN)), Nat.Nat (Nat.N subbed atLeastSecondSubbed_ (Nat.Is aPlusDifference Nat.To aPlusN) (Nat.Is bPlusDifference Nat.To bPlusN)) ) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N n atLeastN (Nat.Is a Nat.To aPlusN) (Nat.Is b Nat.To bPlusN)) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.N difference atLeastDifference (Nat.Is a Nat.To aPlusDifference) (Nat.Is b Nat.To bPlusDifference))

name: Nat

unions.name: AtMostOrAbove
unions.comment:
The result of comparing a `Nat` to another `Nat`.

  - `EqualOrLess`: <= that `Nat`

  - `Above`: > that `Nat`

Values exist for each condition.

unions.name: BelowOrAtLeast
unions.comment:
The result of comparing a `Nat` to another `Nat`.

  - `EqualOrGreater`: >= that `Nat`

  - `Below`: < that `Nat`

Values exist for each condition.

unions.name: BelowOrInOrAboveRange
unions.comment:
The result of comparing a `Nat` to a range from a lower to an upper bound.

  - `InRange`

  - `AboveRange`: greater than the upper bound

  - `BelowRange`: less than the lower bound?

Values exist for each condition.

unions.name: LessOrEqualOrGreater
unions.comment:
The result of comparing a `Nat` to another `Nat`.

  - `Equal` to that `Nat`

  - `Less` than that `Nat`

  - `Greater` than that `Nat`

Values exist for each condition.

aliases.name: ArgIn
aliases.comment:
`ArgIn minimum maximum ifN_`: An argument somewhere within a `minimum` & `maximum`.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

Note: `max` >= `min` for every existing `Nat (ArgIn min max ...)`:

    percent : Nat (ArgIn min_ Nat100 ifN_) -> Percent

→ `min <= Nat100`

If you want a number where you just care about the minimum, leave the `max` as a type _variable_.

       ↓ minimum    ↓ maximum or  →
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓...

Any natural number:

    Nat (ArgIn min_ max_ ifN_)

A number, at least 5:

    Nat (ArgIn (Nat5Plus minMinus5_) max_ ifN_)

  - `max_` could be a maximum value if there is one

  - `ifN_` could contain extra information if the argument is a `Nat (N ...)`

aliases.name: In
aliases.comment:
`In minimum maximum`: A value somewhere within a `minimum` & `maximum`. We don't know the exact value, though.

       ↓ minimum   ↓ maximum
    ⨯ [✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓ ✓] ⨯ ⨯ ⨯...

Do **not** use it as an argument type.

A number between 3 and 5

    Nat (In Nat3 (Nat5Plus a_))

aliases.name: Is
aliases.comment:
`Is a To b`: an exact value as the diffference `b - a`.

    N Nat5
        (Nat5Plus a_)
        (Is myAge To sistersAge)
        (Is mothersAge To fathersAge)

  - `myAge + 5 = sistersAge`
  - `mothersAge + 5 = fathersAge`

aliases.name: Min
aliases.comment:
Only **value / return types should be `Min`**.

Sometimes, you simply cannot compute a maximum.

    abs : Int -> Nat (In Nat0 ??)

This is where to use `Min`.

    abs : Int -> Nat (Min Nat0)

A number >= 5 for example:

    Nat (Min Nat5)

Every `Min min` is of type `In min`.

aliases.name: N
aliases.comment:
The most detailed description of an exact value.

[`InNat.add`](InNat#add) for example uses this knowledg to describe the number as differences between other type variables.

    add :
        Nat
            (N
                added_
                atLeastAdded_
                (Is min To sumMin)
                (Is max To sumMax)
            )
        -> Nat (ArgIn min max ifN_)
        -> Nat (In sumMin sumMax)

You can just ignore the second difference if you don't need it ([`MinNat.add`](MinNat#add)).

    add :
        Nat
            (N
                added_
                atLeastAdded_
                (Is min To sumMin)
                is_
            )
        -> Nat (ArgIn min max_ ifN_)
        -> Nat (Min sumMin)

aliases.name: Nat
aliases.comment:
A **bounded** natural number (`>= 0`).


### argument-only type

    -- >= 4
    Nat (ArgIn (Nat4Plus minMinus4) max ifN_)

    -- 4 <= nat <= 15
    Nat (ArgIn (Nat4Plus minMinus4) Nat15 ifN_)

    -- any, just >= 0
    Nat range


### value types

    -- >= 4
    Nat (Min Nat4)

    -- 2 <= nat <= 12
    Nat (In Nat2 (Nat12Plus a_))


### storage types

Like what to store in the `Model` for example.

    -- >= 4
    Nat (Min Nat4)

    -- 2 <= nat <= 12
    Nat (In Nat2 Nat12)


### n

    -- = 3, & 3, described as 2 differences
    Nat
        (N Nat3
            (Nat3Plus orMore_)
            (Is a To (Nat3Plus a))
            (Is b To (Nat3Plus b))
        )

    -- An exact number nTo15 away from 15
    Nat (N n atLeastN_ (Is nTo15 To Nat15) is_)

aliases.name: Only
aliases.comment:
Expect an exact number.

Only useful as an **argument & storage** type in combination with [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/)s,
not with `Nat`s.

    byte : Arr (Only Nat8) Bit -> Byte

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/) must have _exactly 8_ `Bit`s.

    type alias TicTacToeBoard =
        Arr
            (Only Nat3)
            (Arr (Only Nat3) TicTacToField)

→ A given [`Arr`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/) must have _exactly 3 by 3_ `TicTacToeField`s.

aliases.name: To
aliases.comment:
Only at type-level in [Is](Nat#Is).

    Is a To b

→ distance `b - a`.

values.name: abs
values.comment:
The absolute value of an `Int`, which is `>= 0`.

    Nat.abs 16 --> Nat 16

    Nat.abs -4 --> Nat 4

Really only use this if you want the absolute value.

    badLength list =
        List.length >> Nat.abs

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                MinNat.add nat1
                    >> Nat.lowerMin nat0
            )
            (nat0 |> MinNat.value)

If something like this isn't possible, [`MinNat.intAtLeast`](MinNat#intAtLeast) is the best way!
values.type: Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Min N.Nat0)

values.name: atMost
values.comment:
**Cap** the `Nat` to at most a number.

    between5And15
        |> Nat.atMost nat10 { lowest = nat5 }
    --> : Nat (In Nat5 (Nat10Plus a_))

    atLeast5 |> Nat.atMost nat10 { lowest = nat5 }
    --> : Nat (In Nat5 (Nat10Plus a_))

`lowest` can be a number <= the minimum.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minNewMax maxNewMax newMaxIfN_) -> { lowest : Nat.Nat (Nat.N lowest atLeastLowest_ (Nat.Is lowestToMin_ Nat.To min) (Nat.Is lowestToMinNewMax_ Nat.To minNewMax)) } -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowest maxNewMax)

values.name: div
values.comment:
Divide (`//`) by a `Nat` >= 1.

  - `/ 0` is impossible

  - `x / d` is at most x

```
atMost7 |> Nat.div nat3
--> Nat 2 : Nat (In Nat0 (Nat7Plus a_))
```
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (N.Nat1Plus divMinMinus1_) divMax_ divIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min_ max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In N.Nat0 max)

values.name: intAtLeast
values.comment:
A `Nat (Min ...)` from an `Int`; if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned.

    9 |> Nat.intAtLeast nat3
    --> Nat 9 : Nat (Min Nat3)

    0 |> Nat.intAtLeast nat3
    --> Nat 3 : Nat (Min Nat3)

You can also use this if you know an `Int` must be at least `minimum`.

But avoid it if you can do better, like

    goodLength =
        List.foldl
            (\_ ->
                MinNat.add nat1
                    >> Nat.lowerMin nat0
            )
            (MinNat.value nat0)

If you want to handle the case `< minimum` yourself, use [`Nat.isIntAtLeast`](Nat#isIntAtLeast).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ lowerIfN_) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Min min)

values.name: intInRange
values.comment:
Create a `Nat (In ...)` by **clamping** an `Int` between a minimum & maximum.

  - if the `Int < minimum`, `minimum` is returned
  - if the `Int > maximum`, `maximum` is returned

```
9 |> Nat.intInRange nat3 nat12
--> Nat 9 : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat12Plus a_))

0 |> Nat.intInRange nat3 nat12
--> Nat 3 : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat12Plus a_))

99 |> Nat.intInRange nat3 nat12
--> Nat 12 : Nat (In Nat3 (Nat12Plus a_))
```

If you want to handle the cases `< minimum` & `> maximum` explicitly, use [`isIntInRange`](Nat#isIntInRange).
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound minUpperBound lowerBoundIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minUpperBound maxUpperBound upperBoundIfN_) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In minLowerBound maxUpperBound)

values.name: isIntAtLeast
values.comment:
If the `Int >= a minimum`, `Just` the `Nat (Min minimum)`, else `Nothing`.

    4 |> Nat.isIntAtLeast nat5
    --> Nothing : Maybe (Nat (Min Nat5))

    1234 |> Nat.isIntAtLeast nat5
    --> Just (Nat 1234) : Maybe (Nat (Min Nat5))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ lowerIfN_) -> Basics.Int -> Maybe.Maybe (Nat.Nat (Nat.Min min))

values.name: isIntInRange
values.comment:
Compared to a range from a lower to an upper bound, is the `Int` `BelowOrInOrAboveRange`?

    rejectOrAcceptUserInt :
        Int
        -> Result String (Nat (In Nat1 (Nat100Plus a_)))
    rejectOrAcceptUserInt int =
        case int |> Nat.isIntInRange nat1 nat100 of
            InRange inRange ->
                Ok inRange

            BelowRange () ->
                Err "must be >= 1"

            AboveRange _ ->
                Err "must be <= 100"


    rejectOrAcceptUserInt 0
    --> Err "must be >= 1"
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minLowerBound minUpperBound lowerBoundIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn minUpperBound maxUpperBound upperBoundIfN_) -> Basics.Int -> Nat.BelowOrInOrAboveRange () (Nat.Nat (Nat.In minLowerBound maxUpperBound)) (Nat.Nat (Nat.Min (N.Nat1Plus maxUpperBound)))

values.name: lowerMin
values.comment:
Set the minimum lower.

    [ atLeast3, atLeast4 ]

Elm complains:

> But all the previous elements in the list are: `Nat (Min Nat3)`

    [ atLeast3
    , atLeast4 |> Nat.lowerMin nat3
    ]
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn lowerMin min lowerIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowerMin max)

values.name: mul
values.comment:
Multiply by a `Nat` >= 1.
we know that if `a >= 1`, `x * a >= x`.

    atLeast5  |> Nat.mul nat2
    --> Nat 10 : Nat (Min Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> Nat.mul nat5
    --> Nat 10 : Nat (Min Nat2)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (N.Nat1Plus mulMinMinus1_) mulMax_ mulIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Min min)

values.name: random
values.comment:
Generate a random `Nat` in a range.

    Nat.random nat1 nat10
    --> Random.Generator (Nat (In Nat1 (Nat10Plus a_)))
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn lowerBoundMin upperBoundMin lowerBoundIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn upperBoundMin upperBoundMax upperBoundIfN_) -> Random.Generator (Nat.Nat (Nat.In lowerBoundMin upperBoundMax))

values.name: range
values.comment:
`Nat`s from a first to last value.

    Nat.range nat3 nat10
    --> : List (Nat (In Nat3 (Nat10Plus a_)))

    Nat.range nat3 atLeast10
    --> : List (Nat (Min Nat3))

The resulting `List` always has >= 1 element.

With [nats in typesafe-array](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/Arr#nats) the type even knows the length! Try it.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn firstMin lastMin firstIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn lastMin lastMax lastIfN_) -> List.List (Nat.Nat (Nat.In firstMin lastMax))

values.name: remainderBy
values.comment:
The remainder after dividing by a `Nat` >= 1. `x |> remainderBy d` is at most `d`

    atMost7 |> Nat.remainderBy nat3
    --> Nat 1 : Nat (In Nat0 (Nat3Plus a_))

In theory, `x % d` should be at most `d - 1`, but this can't be expressed well by the type.
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (N.Nat1Plus divMinMinus1_) divMax divIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min_ max_ ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.In N.Nat0 divMax)

values.name: restoreMax
values.comment:
Make it fit into functions with require a higher maximum.

You should design type annotations as general as possible.

    onlyAtMost18 : Nat (ArgIn min Nat18 ifN_)

    onlyAtMost18 between3And8 -- fine

But once you implement `onlyAtMost18`, you might use the value in `onlyAtMost19`.

    onlyAtMost18 value =
        -- onlyAtMost19 value -- error
        onlyAtMost19
            (value |> Nat.restoreMax nat18)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn max newMax newMaxIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max ifN) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min newMax ifN)

values.name: theGreater
values.comment:
The greater of 2 `Nat`s.

    Nat.theGreater
        between1And3
        (atLeast4 |> Nat.lowerMin nat1)
    --> : Nat (Min Nat1)
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: theSmaller
values.comment:
The smaller of 2 `Nat`s.

    Nat.theSmaller
        (nat3 |> MinNat.value)
        (atLeast4 |> Nat.lowerMin nat3)
    --> Nat 3 : Nat (Min Nat3)
values.type: Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range -> Nat.Nat range

values.name: toPower
values.comment:
The `Nat ^ a Nat >= 1`.
We know that if `a >= 1  →  x ^ a >= x`

    atLeast5 |> Nat.toPower nat2
    --> Nat 25 : Nat (Min Nat5)

    atLeast2 |> Nat.toPower nat5
    --> Nat 25 : Nat (Min Nat2)
values.type: Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn (N.Nat1Plus powMinMinus1_) powMax_ powIfN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.ArgIn min max_ ifN_) -> Nat.Nat (Nat.Min min)

name: TypeNats
